2719:func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {

2715	// TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected verifies that when execute-bead
2716	// invokes the embedded-agent harness, its session/telemetry runtime state is
2717	// redirected into a DDx-owned directory inside the execution bundle instead
2718	// of being written at the worktree root. Regression guard for ddx-cba2dc64.
2719	func TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected(t *testing.T) {
2720		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2721			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
2722			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
2723		}
2724	
2725		// Snapshot of wtPath root contents captured during Run so the assertion
2726		// can run before ExecuteBead removes the worktree on return.
2727		var wtPathDuringRun string
2728		var wtRootBefore []string
2729		var wtRootAfter []string
2730		var sessionLogDirSeen string
2731		simulatedSessionFile := "agent-embedded-session.jsonl"
2732	
2733		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{
2734			result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "agent"},
2735			sideEffect: func(opts agent.RunOptions) error {
2736				wtPathDuringRun = opts.WorkDir
2737				sessionLogDirSeen = opts.SessionLogDir
2738	
2739				// Capture worktree root listing before simulating writes.
2740				entries, err := os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2741				if err != nil {
2742					return err
2743				}
2744				for _, e := range entries {
2745					wtRootBefore = append(wtRootBefore, e.Name())
2746				}
2747	
2748				// Simulate the embedded-agent harness writing runtime state.
2749				// It MUST land in opts.SessionLogDir, not opts.WorkDir. If the
2750				// execute-bead wiring is broken and SessionLogDir is empty or the
2751				// worktree root, this write will land at the worktree root and
2752				// the post-check below will catch it.
2753				if opts.SessionLogDir == "" {
2754					return fmt.Errorf("embedded agent runner received empty SessionLogDir; runtime state would land at worktree root")
2755				}
2756				if err := os.MkdirAll(opts.SessionLogDir, 0o755); err != nil {
2757					return err
2758				}
2759				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(opts.SessionLogDir, simulatedSessionFile), []byte(`{"event":"started"}`+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
2760					return err
2761				}
2762	
2763				// Capture worktree root listing after simulated writes.
2764				entries, err = os.ReadDir(opts.WorkDir)
2765				if err != nil {
2766					return err
2767				}
2768				for _, e := range entries {
2769					wtRootAfter = append(wtRootAfter, e.Name())
2770				}
2771				return nil
2772			},
2773		}
2774		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
2775	
2776		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead", "--harness", "agent")
2777		require.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome, "execute-bead should succeed for this test")
2778	
2779		// The runner must have received a SessionLogDir override.
2780		require.NotEmpty(t, sessionLogDirSeen, "execute-bead must pass a SessionLogDir to the embedded harness")
2781	
2782		// The override must point inside the tracked execution bundle, not at
2783		// the worktree root.
2784		require.NotEmpty(t, res.ExecutionDir, "execute-bead must record an execution bundle dir")
2785		bundleAbs := filepath.Join(f.WorkingDir, filepath.FromSlash(res.ExecutionDir))
2786		absLog, err := filepath.Abs(sessionLogDirSeen)
2787		require.NoError(t, err)
2788		absBundle, err := filepath.Abs(bundleAbs)
2789		require.NoError(t, err)
2790		assert.Truef(t, strings.HasPrefix(absLog, absBundle+string(filepath.Separator)),
2791			"SessionLogDir (%s) must be inside the execution bundle (%s)", absLog, absBundle)
2792		assert.Equal(t, "embedded", filepath.Base(absLog),
2793			"SessionLogDir must be the bundle's embedded/ subdirectory")
2794	
2795		// The worktree root must not gain any files during the run.
2796		require.NotEmpty(t, wtPathDuringRun)
2797		assert.Equal(t, wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter,
2798			"worktree root entries must not change while the embedded harness runs (before=%v after=%v)",
2799			wtRootBefore, wtRootAfter)
2800		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, simulatedSessionFile,
2801			"simulated embedded session file must not land at the worktree root")
2802		assert.NotContains(t, wtRootAfter, ".agent-session.json",
2803			"embedded agent must not write .agent-session.json at the worktree root")
2804	
2805		// The simulated session file must exist at the redirected location.
2806		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(sessionLogDirSeen, simulatedSessionFile))
2807	}

2809	// TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged verifies that the synthesized execute-bead
2810	// prompt is emitted as a well-structured XML document with the tags required
2811	// by FEAT-006's Prompt Rationalizer Contract. It also guards against regression
2812	// to the old markdown-heading-only prompt structure.
2813	func TestExecuteBeadPromptIsXMLTagged(t *testing.T) {
2814		workDir := t.TempDir()
2815		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
2816			ID:          "xml-bead",
2817			Title:       "Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template",
2818			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
2819			Priority:    0,
2820			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
2821			Parent:      "ddx-parent",
2822			Description: "Replace the markdown-heading prompt with an XML-tagged structure so downstream tooling can diff and validate sections deterministically.",
2823			Acceptance:  "Prompt is XML-tagged with <execute-bead>, <bead>, <governing>, and <instructions>.",
2824			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "area:docs"},
2825			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-XML-TEST"},
2826		})
2827		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feat-xml.md")
2828		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
2829		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
2830	ddx:
2831	  id: FEAT-XML-TEST
2832	  title: XML Test Spec
2833	---
2834	# XML Test Spec
2835	`), 0o644))
2836	
2837		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
2838			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111cafe",
2839			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222beef",
2840		}
2841		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
2842		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
2843		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
2844		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
2845		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
2846		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
2847		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
2848	
2849		_ = runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "xml-bead")
2850	
2851		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
2852		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
2853		require.NoError(t, err)
2854		promptText := string(promptRaw)
2855	
2856		// Required root and subsection tags.
2857		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<execute-bead>")
2858		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</execute-bead>")
2859		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<bead id="xml-bead">`)
2860		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</bead>")
2861		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<title>Adopt XML-tagged execute-bead prompt template</title>")
2862		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<description>")
2863		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</description>")
2864		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<acceptance>")
2865		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</acceptance>")
2866		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<labels>area:agent, area:docs</labels>")
2867		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `parent="ddx-parent"`)
2868		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `spec-id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
2869		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `base-rev="aaaa1111cafe"`)
2870		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<metadata `)
2871		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<governing>")
2872		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</governing>")
2873		assert.Contains(t, promptText, `<ref id="FEAT-XML-TEST"`)
2874		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "<instructions>")
2875		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "</instructions>")
2876	
2877		// Regression guard: no markdown-heading-only sections.
2878		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "# Execute Bead\n")
2879		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Bead\n")
2880		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Description\n")
2881		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Acceptance Criteria\n")
2882		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Governing References\n")
2883		assert.NotContains(t, promptText, "## Execution Rules\n")
2884	
2885		// The prompt must be parseable as a well-formed XML document.
2886		decoder := xml.NewDecoder(bytes.NewBufferString(promptText))
2887		for {
2888			_, tokErr := decoder.Token()
2889			if tokErr == io.EOF {
2890				break
2891			}
2892			require.NoError(t, tokErr, "prompt must be well-formed XML: %s", promptText)
2893		}
2894	}

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"os/exec"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"strconv"
10		"strings"
11		"sync"
12		"testing"
13	
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
16	)
17	
18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	// Git helpers
20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	
22	// scrubbedGitEnvInteg returns the current environment with all GIT_* variables
23	// removed, ensuring test-local git subprocesses don't inherit parent repo state.
24	func scrubbedGitEnvInteg() []string {
25		parent := os.Environ()
26		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
27		for _, kv := range parent {
28			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
29				continue
30			}
31			env = append(env, kv)
32		}
33		return env
34	}
35	
36	// runGitInteg runs a git command in dir with scrubbed GIT_* env.
37	// Fails the test on non-zero exit.
38	func runGitInteg(t *testing.T, dir string, args ...string) string {
39		t.Helper()
40		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
41		cmd.Dir = dir
42		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
43		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
44		if err != nil {
45			t.Fatalf("git %v in %s: %v\n%s", args, dir, err, out)
46		}
47		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
48	}
49	
50	// runGitIntegOutput runs a git command and returns (output, error) — for cases
51	// where failure is expected or handled by the caller.
52	func runGitIntegOutput(dir string, args ...string) (string, error) {
53		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
54		cmd.Dir = dir
55		cmd.Env = scrubbedGitEnvInteg()
56		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
57		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err
58	}
59	
60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
61	// Repo setup
62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	
64	// newScriptHarnessRepo creates a temp git repo with an initial seed commit on
65	// main, seeds .ddx/beads.jsonl with beadCount open beads (IDs like
66	// ddx-int-0001, ddx-int-0002 …), and returns (projectRoot, initialSHA).
67	// The initialSHA is the SHA immediately after the "chore: initial seed" commit,
68	// BEFORE the seed-beads commit, so callers can measure commits added beyond it.
69	func newScriptHarnessRepo(t *testing.T, beadCount int) (string, string) {
70		t.Helper()
71	
72		root := t.TempDir()
73	
74		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
75		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
76		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
77	
78		// Create initial seed file and commit so the repo has a HEAD.
79		seedFile := filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt")
80		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(seedFile, []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
81		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
82		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
83	
84		initialSHA := runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
85	
86		// Set up .ddx dir and bead store.
87		ddxDir := filepath.Join(root, ".ddx")
88		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0755))
89		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
90		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
91	
92		for i := 0; i < beadCount; i++ {
93			id := fmt.Sprintf("ddx-int-%04d", i+1)
94			b := &bead.Bead{
95				ID:        id,
96				Title:     fmt.Sprintf("Integration test bead %d", i+1),
97				IssueType: "task",
98				Priority:  i,
99			}
100			require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
101		}
102	
103		// Commit beads.jsonl so the initial worktree contains it.
104		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
105		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: seed beads")
106	
107		return root, initialSHA
108	}
109	
110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	// Directive file helper
112	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
113	
114	// writeDirectiveFile writes a directive file at path with the given lines.
115	func writeDirectiveFile(t *testing.T, path string, lines []string) {
116		t.Helper()
117		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755))
118		content := strings.Join(lines, "\n") + "\n"
119		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644))
120	}
121	
122	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
123	// Executor helpers
124	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	
126	// landSerializerMu guards landSerializerMap.
127	var (
128		landSerializerMu  sync.Mutex
129		landSerializerMap = map[string]*sync.Mutex{}
130	)
131	
132	// landMutexFor returns the per-projectRoot mutex that serializes Land() calls.
133	// This mimics the production per-project LandCoordinator goroutine
134	// (cli/internal/server/workers.go). Tests that spawn concurrent workers share
135	// this mutex so the CAS-based UpdateRefTo in Land() never races.
136	func landMutexFor(projectRoot string) *sync.Mutex {
137		landSerializerMu.Lock()
138		defer landSerializerMu.Unlock()
139		m, ok := landSerializerMap[projectRoot]
140		if !ok {
141			m = &sync.Mutex{}
142			landSerializerMap[projectRoot] = m
143		}
144		return m
145	}
146	
147	// scriptHarnessExecutor builds an ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc that:
148	//  1. Runs ExecuteBead with the script harness (directive file = directivePath).
149	//  2. Passes the result through LandBeadResult with a LandingAdvancer that
150	//     calls Land() — serialized via landMutexFor(projectRoot).
151	//
152	// Both ExecuteBead and Land() are serialized per projectRoot. This is required
153	// because ExecuteBead calls CommitTracker (which acquires the git index lock)
154	// and because Land() uses CAS-based UpdateRefTo. In production, the server
155	// dispatches one bead per worker and serializes Land() via the LandCoordinator
156	// goroutine; in integration tests, the same mutex handles both.
157	//
158	// Failed exits (exit code != 0) with commits are preserved under
159	// refs/ddx/iterations/ by LandBeadResult before Land() is invoked.
160	func scriptHarnessExecutor(t *testing.T, projectRoot, directivePath string) ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc {
161		t.Helper()
162		// Build Runner once at executor-construction time so concurrent goroutine
163		// calls do not race on BuiltinCatalog (NewRunner is not goroutine-safe
164		// during catalog construction).
165		runner := NewRunner(Config{})
166		gitOps := &RealGitOps{}
167		orchGitOps := &RealOrchestratorGitOps{}
168		// Per-projectRoot mutex serializes git operations so concurrent workers
169		// don't race on the git index or on Land()'s CAS UpdateRefTo.
170		repoMu := landMutexFor(projectRoot)
171	
172		return ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
173			repoMu.Lock()
174			defer repoMu.Unlock()
175	
176			res, err := ExecuteBead(ctx, projectRoot, beadID, ExecuteBeadOptions{
177				Harness:     "script",
178				Model:       directivePath,
179				AgentRunner: runner,
180			}, gitOps)
181			if err != nil {
182				if res != nil {
183					return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
184				}
185				return ExecuteBeadReport{
186					BeadID: beadID,
187					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
188					Detail: err.Error(),
189				}, nil
190			}
191	
192			landing, landErr := LandBeadResult(projectRoot, res, orchGitOps, BeadLandingOptions{
193				LandingAdvancer: func(r *ExecuteBeadResult) (*LandResult, error) {
194					// landMutexFor is already held (repoMu == landMutexFor(projectRoot)).
195					req := BuildLandRequestFromResult(projectRoot, r)
196					return Land(projectRoot, req, RealLandingGitOps{})
197				},
198			})
199			if landErr != nil {
200				return ExecuteBeadReport{
201					BeadID: beadID,
202					Status: ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
203					Detail: landErr.Error(),
204				}, nil
205			}
206			ApplyLandingToResult(res, landing)
207			return executeBeadResultToReport(res), nil
208		})
209	}
210	
211	// executeBeadResultToReport converts ExecuteBeadResult to ExecuteBeadReport.
212	func executeBeadResultToReport(res *ExecuteBeadResult) ExecuteBeadReport {
213		return ExecuteBeadReport{
214			BeadID:             res.BeadID,
215			AttemptID:          res.AttemptID,
216			WorkerID:           res.WorkerID,
217			Harness:            res.Harness,
218			Model:              res.Model,
219			Status:             res.Status,
220			Detail:             res.Detail,
221			SessionID:          res.SessionID,
222			BaseRev:            res.BaseRev,
223			ResultRev:          res.ResultRev,
224			PreserveRef:        res.PreserveRef,
225			NoChangesRationale: res.NoChangesRationale,
226		}
227	}

229	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
230	// Git assertion helpers
231	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	
233	// gitCommitCount returns the number of commits reachable from ref (plus any
234	// additional git rev-list args like "--not", "SHA").
235	func gitCommitCount(t *testing.T, projectRoot string, refAndArgs ...string) int {
236		t.Helper()
237		args := append([]string{"rev-list", "--count"}, refAndArgs...)
238		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, args...)
239		n, err := strconv.Atoi(out)
240		require.NoError(t, err, "git rev-list --count %v", refAndArgs)
241		return n
242	}
243	
244	// gitHasMergeCommits returns true when any merge commits exist in ref's history.
245	func gitHasMergeCommits(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
246		t.Helper()
247		out := runGitInteg(t, projectRoot, "log", "--merges", "--format=%H", ref)
248		return out != ""
249	}
250	
251	// refExists returns true when the given git ref is present in the repo.
252	func refExists(t *testing.T, projectRoot, ref string) bool {
253		t.Helper()
254		_, err := runGitIntegOutput(projectRoot, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
255		return err == nil
256	}
257	
258	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	// Store helpers
260	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	// makeLoopStore creates an ExecuteBeadLoopStore backed by a bead.Store rooted
263	// in ddxDir. The store is already initialised by newScriptHarnessRepo.
264	func makeLoopStore(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) ExecuteBeadLoopStore {
265		t.Helper()
266		return bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
267	}
268	
269	// countClosedBeads counts how many beads in ddxDir have status "closed".
270	func countClosedBeads(t *testing.T, ddxDir string) int {
271		t.Helper()
272		store := bead.NewStore(ddxDir)
273		all, err := store.List("", "", nil)
274		require.NoError(t, err)
275		n := 0
276		for _, b := range all {
277			if b.Status == bead.StatusClosed {
278				n++
279			}
280		}
281		return n
282	}
283	
284	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
285	// Shell quoting helper
286	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
287	
288	// escapeForShell escapes a string for embedding in a single-quoted shell argument.
289	func escapeForShell(s string) string {
290		return strings.ReplaceAll(s, "'", `'\''`)
291	}

@@ -2643,24 +2643,70 @@ func TestExecuteBeadGateBlocksLanding(t *testing.T) {
 
 // TestExecuteBeadNoGatesWhenNoChanges verifies that gates are not evaluated
 // when the agent produces no changes (resultRev == baseRev).
+//
+// Migrated off fakeExecuteBeadGit / fakeAgentRunner per concerns.md §testing
+// ("no mocks, period"; "never mock the thing you are testing"). Exercises the
+// real ExecuteBead → LandBeadResult → Land pipeline against an isolated real
+// git repo, with the script harness driven by a per-attempt directive file
+// that emits zero commits — the real no-changes signal. Parent: ddx-d9df348d.
 func TestExecuteBeadNoGatesWhenNoChanges(t *testing.T) {
-	git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
-		mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
-		wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // same rev = no changes
+	workDir := t.TempDir()
+
+	scrubEnv := func() []string {
+		parent := os.Environ()
+		env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
+		for _, kv := range parent {
+			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
+				continue
+			}
+			env = append(env, kv)
+		}
+		return env
+	}
+	runGit := func(args ...string) string {
+		t.Helper()
+		cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
+		cmd.Dir = workDir
+		cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
+		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
+		require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
+		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
 	}
-	runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
-	f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
 
-	seedExecuteBead(t, f.WorkingDir, &bead.Bead{
+	runGit("init", "-b", "main")
+	runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
+	runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
+	runGit("add", ".")
+	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
+
+	seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
 		ID:        "gate-bead-nochange",
 		Title:     "Bead with gate but no changes",
 		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
 		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
 		Extra:     map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-GATE-TEST"},
 	})
-	seedGateDocs(t, f.WorkingDir, []string{"false"})
+	// Gate docs must be committed so the real worker worktree (created at
+	// baseSHA) sees them.
+	seedGateDocs(t, workDir, []string{"false"})
+	runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl", "docs")
+	runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead and gate docs")
+	baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
+
+	// Directive file drives the script harness to emit no commits — the real
+	// signal that the agent produced no changes. Replaces fakeAgentRunner's
+	// canned Result struct + mainHeadRev==wtHeadRev.
+	directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
+	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte("no-op\n"), 0o644))
 
-	res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "gate-bead-nochange")
+	// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
+	f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
+	f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
+
+	res := runExecuteBead(t, f, nil, "gate-bead-nochange",
+		"--from", baseSHA,
+		"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
 
 	assert.Equal(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome)
 	assert.Empty(t, res.GateResults, "gates must not run when agent made no changes")

input.command:
git add cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead_test.go cli/internal/agent/script.go && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected to real git + script harness [ddx-c55119a7]

Replaces fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with an isolated real-git
repo and the script harness driven by a directive file. The directive
brackets a simulated embedded-agent write to $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR with
ls -A snapshots of the worker worktree root, so the end-to-end wiring
(ExecuteBead -> RunOptions.SessionLogDir -> runner) is exercised through
real git and real Land() rather than a canned Result + same-rev pair.

The script harness now interpolates $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR from
opts.SessionLogDir; without it, directives cannot faithfully simulate a
harness that writes session/telemetry state to the override dir.

Parent: ddx-d9df348d. concerns.md §testing: no mocks, period.
EOF
)"

input.command:
CGO_ENABLED=1 git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(cmd): migrate TestExecuteBeadEmbeddedAgentStateRedirected to real git + script harness [ddx-c55119a7]

Replaces fakeExecuteBeadGit + fakeAgentRunner with an isolated real-git
repo and the script harness driven by a directive file. The directive
brackets a simulated embedded-agent write to $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR with
ls -A snapshots of the worker worktree root, so the end-to-end wiring
(ExecuteBead -> RunOptions.SessionLogDir -> runner) is exercised through
real git and real Land() rather than a canned Result + same-rev pair.

The script harness now interpolates $DDX_SESSION_LOG_DIR from
opts.SessionLogDir; without it, directives cannot faithfully simulate a
harness that writes session/telemetry state to the override dir.

Parent: ddx-d9df348d. concerns.md §testing: no mocks, period.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -40
